prs.title: ci: automate PR feedback when CI lint/build/test checks fail

labels.name: status: needs review
labels.description: The pull request is ready for maintainer review

prs.body:
Re-implements the PR-helper CI feedback flow as a scheduled cron driver
  instead of a workflow_run listener. The previous workflow_run-based approach
  (PR #1637) was reverted in PR #1638 because workflow_run was flagged as an
  unsafe trigger pattern.
  
  The new on-ci-result workflow runs every 10 minutes, lists open PRs, and for
  each one looks up the latest "PR Checks" run by head SHA. It updates the
  dashboard CI section, force-swaps the status label to "needs revision" on
  failure, and posts a one-time @mention notification on clean->failure
  transitions. Runs entirely in base-repo context, so fork PRs are handled
  without exposing any fork-controlled input plane.
  
  **Description**:
  
  Automate PR feedback when CI lint/build/test checks fail, using a scheduled cron driver that reconciles the dashboard, status label, and
  contributor ping in base-repo context.
  
  * Add `helpers/ci.js` with `getLatestPRChecksRun`, `classifyWorkflowFailure` (lint / build / tests via job + step inspection), and
  `hadPriorCIFailure` (transition detection)
  * Add `bot-on-ci-result.js` cron driver that iterates open PRs, skips drafts and bot-authored PRs, and per PR: re-renders the dashboard CI section,
  force-swaps the status label to `status: needs revision` on failure, and posts a one-time @mention notification only on clean->failure transitions
  * Add `.github/workflows/on-ci-result.yaml` with `schedule: cron '*/10 * * * *'` plus `workflow_dispatch`, hardened runner, default-branch
  checkout, and a global concurrency group so two ticks cannot double-post
  * Extend `helpers/comments.js` with `buildCISection`, `buildCIFailureNotificationComment`, and a greeting that explains the `status: needs review`
  vs `status: needs revision` semantics
  * Wire the new helper module into `helpers/index.js`
  * Patch `runAllChecksAndComment` in `helpers/api.js` to accept and propagate the precomputed `ci` result through to `buildBotComment`
  
  **Related issue(s)**:
  
  Fixes #1631

prs.title: Refactor /assign command to separate decision from effects

labels.name: status: needs review
labels.description: The pull request is ready for maintainer review

prs.body:
Refactor the /assign command to separate eligibility decisions from GitHub mutations, making the code easier to test, reason about, and migrate into a reusable automation service.

* Extract assignment eligibility logic into pure decision function
* Create structured AssignmentOutcome enum for all decision results
* Separate GitHub mutations into dedicated effect executor function
* Add unit tests for decision layer without GitHub write operations
* Preserve existing assignment behavior and TOCTOU protection
* Maintain fresh-fetch validation and skill-level-changed detection

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1626 

**Notes for reviewer**:

This refactoring introduces a two-layer architecture:

1. **Decision Layer** (\`determineAssignmentEligibility\`): Runs all eligibility checks and returns a structured decision object without performing any GitHub mutations. This makes the logic testable in isolation.

2. **Effect Layer** (\`executeAssignmentEffects\`): Takes the decision object and applies appropriate GitHub side effects (comments, assignments, labels).

**Key Points:**
- All 42 existing integration tests pass unchanged
- Added 10 new unit tests for the decision layer (\`test-assign-decision-layer.js\`)

labels.name: status: needs review
labels.description: The pull request is ready for maintainer review

prs.body:
**Description**:

Add Linux Clang+libc++ build configuration to test stdlib portability on self-hosted Linux runners, providing a faster and cheaper alternative to macOS builds for catching libc++-specific issues.

* Add custom vcpkg triplet `x64-linux-clang-libcxx` in `vcpkg-triplets/`
* Add CMake configure presets `linux-x64-clang-libcxx-debug` and `linux-x64-clang-libcxx-release`
* Add CMake build presets for both debug and release configurations
* Add `build-linux-clang-libcxx` job to `zxc-build-library.yaml` workflow
* Add `run-clang-libcxx-build` input flag to workflow (defaults to false)
* Enable libc++ build in nightly workflow via `on-schedule-builds.yaml`
* Document libc++ build requirements and usage in `README.md`

**Related issue(s)**:

Fixes #1620 

### Technical Implementation

**Custom vcpkg Triplet** (`vcpkg-triplets/x64-linux-clang-libcxx.cmake`):
- Configures vcpkg to build all C++ dependencies with Clang and libc++
- Sets compiler flags: `-stdlib=libc++`
- Sets linker flags: `-stdlib=libc++ -lc++abi`
- Ensures ABI compatibility across gRPC, Protobuf, abseil, log4cxx, and all other C++ dependencies

**CMake Presets**:
- Inherit from `vcpkg-base` for consistency
- Override compiler to Clang
- Add libc++ flags to compilation and linking
- Point to custom triplet via `VCPKG_OVERLAY_TRIPLETS`

**CI Integration**:
- New job runs only when `run-clang-libcxx-build: true` is set
- Enabled in nightly workflow, disabled for PR builds
- Installs `libc++-dev` and `libc++abi-dev` packages
- Builds and runs unit tests for both Debug and Release configurations

prs.title: Fix: Near-miss command detection ignores non-whitespace separators
prs.body: Fix: Near-miss regex fails to detect non-whitespace separators Fixes #1537 Description The near-miss command detection in bot-on-comment.js used \s+ in its regex, which only matched commands followed by whitespace. Malformed inputs like /assign!, /assign,oops, or /assign.work — where the command is followed by a non-space character — silently passed through without triggering the correction message. This PR replaces \s+ with the \b (word boundary) assertion, which matches at any position where a word character is adjacent to a non-word character. This catches all malformed command variants, not just whitespace-separated ones.

prs.title: FIX TruncatedSVD: correct power_iteration_normalizer constraint from …
prs.body:
Fixes #33472 

## What and Why

[TruncatedSVD](cci:2://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/sklearn/decomposition/_truncated_svd.py:27:0-321:40) has `power_iteration_normalizer='QR'` documented as a valid value, but the internal `_parameter_constraints` had `'OR'` instead of `'QR'` in its `StrOptions` set. This caused an `InvalidParameterError` to be raised before [fit_transform](cci:1://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/sklearn/decomposition/_truncated_svd.py:208:4-275:28) even ran, even though the user passed the exact value shown in the docs.

## Changes

- [sklearn/decomposition/_truncated_svd.py](cci:7://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/sklearn/decomposition/_truncated_svd.py:0:0-0:0): Fixed typo `'OR'` → `'QR'` in `_parameter_constraints`
- [sklearn/decomposition/tests/test_truncated_svd.py](cci:7://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/sklearn/decomposition/tests/test_truncated_svd.py:0:0-0:0): Added [test_power_iteration_normalizer](cci:1://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/sklearn/decomposition/tests/test_truncated_svd.py:214:0-224:56) parametrized over all valid values (`'auto'`, `'QR'`, `'LU'`, `'none'`) with `algorithm='randomized'`
- [doc/whats_new/upcoming_changes/sklearn.decomposition/33476.fix.rst](cci:7://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/scikit-learn/doc/whats_new/upcoming_changes/sklearn.decomposition/33476.fix.rst:0:0-0:0): Changelog entry

## Reproducer

```python
import numpy as np, sklearn

X = np.random.randn(100, 26)
tsvd = sklearn.decomposition.TruncatedSVD(
    n_components=10, algorithm='randomized', power_iteration_normalizer='QR'
)
X_trans = tsvd.fit_transform(X)  # Previously raised InvalidParameterError

prs.title: Add optional REST API and Web UI for ScrapPY
prs.body:
#12 

### Summary

Introduces an optional, decoupled REST API and web interface for ScrapPY, following OWASP security principles. The core ScrapPY logic remains unmodified.

### Changes

- `scrappy_web/` - New module containing:
  - FastAPI REST API with JWT authentication
  - Minimal static HTML/JS web client
  - 12 pytest tests (all passing)
  - Environment-based configuration
- `Dockerfile` & `docker-compose.yml` - Production-ready containerization
- `.gitignore` - Updated for new artifacts
- `ScrapPY.py` - Fixed PyPDF2 v3.0+ compatibility (deprecated API calls)

### Security Features

| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|---------------|
| Authentication | JWT tokens required for all operations |
| Rate Limiting | Configurable via slowapi (5 login/min, 10 jobs/min) |
| Explicit Consent | Required flag before every scan |
| Input Validation | File size limits (10MB), PDF magic byte verification |
| Isolation | ScrapPY invoked via subprocess, not imported |
| No Unsafe Defaults | Secret key must be set via environment variable |

### Testing

```bash
SCRAPPY_SECRET_KEY="test-key-minimum-32-chars-long" pytest scrappy_web/tests/ -v
# Result: 12 passed



export SCRAPPY_SECRET_KEY=$(python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))")
uvicorn scrappy_web.api.main:app --reload
# Access: http://127.0.0.1:8000/ui/

prs.title: fix: preserve TypeScript compilation errors instead of masking as MOD…
prs.body:
#5555 
When ts-node or similar TypeScript compilers encounter a type error,
the error was being caught and masked by fallback module loading logic,
resulting in unhelpful "Cannot find module" messages instead of the
actual TypeScript diagnostics.

This fix detects TypeScript compilation errors by checking for TS-specific
patterns in error messages and throws them immediately without attempting
fallback mechanisms, preserving the full diagnostic information for users.

Fixes issue where TypeScript errors in imported modules showed as
"Cannot find module" rather than displaying the actual type error.

labels.name: For Uncommitted Bug
labels.description: PR for untriaged, rejected, closed or missing bug

prs.body:
#62780 


Prevents the "Move to file" refactor from appearing when the selection
is inside an unbraced if/else branch (e.g., `if (true) const x = 1;`).
Previously, invoking the refactor would generate invalid code by
extracting only part of a statement.

Added guards at three validation points:
- getAvailableActions: blocks refactor from appearing in UI
- doChange: safety check prevents invalid transformations
- getStatementsToMove: early rejection during statement collection

Statements inside braced blocks remain unaffected and continue to work
correctly.

Fixes invalid code generation when refactoring unbraced control flow.

prs.title: feat(sqlite): add async function support to Database.transaction() Fi…
prs.body:
#24664 
feat(sqlite): add async function support to Database.transaction()

### What does this PR do?
- Add Promise detection and handling in wrapTransaction function
- Maintain transaction isolation during async operations
- Properly commit transactions after async function completion
- Handle both sync errors and async rejections with rollback
- Preserve backward compatibility with synchronous transactions
- Add comprehensive test coverage for async transaction scenarios

Previously, async functions in transactions would commit immediately
after the first await, breaking transaction isolation. Now transactions
properly wait for the entire async function to complete before committing.

## What does this PR accomplish?

**Adds color customization properties to CheckBox component**

The CheckBox component now supports color customization similar to the Switch component. This enhancement adds three new properties:

1. **BoxColorChecked** - Controls the color of the checkbox border/box when checked (default: Blue)
2. **BoxColorUnchecked** - Controls the color of the checkbox border/box when unchecked (default: Gray)  
3. **CheckmarkColor** - Controls the color of the checkmark when checked (default: White)

Users can now customize CheckBox appearance to match their app's design theme, providing visual consistency across their user interface. This addresses the feature disparity between CheckBox and Switch components.

issues.body:
### Jenkins and plugins versions report

[get_last_accessed()](cci:1://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/resources-ai-chatbot-plugin/chatbot-core/api/services/memory.py:190:0-211:15) in [api/services/memory.py](cci:7://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/resources-ai-chatbot-plugin/chatbot-core/api/services/memory.py:0:0-0:0) raises a `TypeError` whenever it's called for a session that exists on disk but isn't loaded in memory — which happens after every server restart.

### What's happening

[load_session()](cci:1://file:///Users/hemant/Desktop/opensource/resources-ai-chatbot-plugin/chatbot-core/api/services/sessionmanager.py:85:0-89:46) returns a list of message dicts (the chat history), but the code on line 211 treats it like a dict and tries to access `history["last_accessed"]`. Subscripting a list with a string key blows up with `TypeError`.

```python
# memory.py line 206–211
history = load_session(session_id)   # returns list[dict]
if not history:
    return None

return history["last_accessed"]      # TypeError: list indices must be integers or slices, not str
```



### What Operating System are you using (both controller, and any agents involved in the problem)?

Mac OS Tahoe 26.3.1

### Reproduction steps


1. 1.Start the server and create a session with at least one message (so it gets persisted)
3. 2.Restart the server (this clears the in-memory _sessions store)
5. 3.Anything that calls _get_last_accessed() for that session will crash

### Expected Results

Should return None for disk-only sessions since the timestamp was never persisted.

### Actual Results

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "api/services/memory.py", line 211, in get_last_accessed
    return history["last_accessed"]
TypeError: list indices must be integers or slices, not str


### Anything else?

_No response_

### Are you interested in contributing a fix?

Yes, If this issue aligns with the repo then I can raise a PR

issues.title: center_of_mass returns zoo/nan when total mass is zero
issues.body:
when run
```python
from sympy import symbols
from sympy.physics.mechanics import Particle
from sympy.physics.vector import Point, ReferenceFrame
from sympy.physics.mechanics.functions import center_of_mass

N = ReferenceFrame('N')
o = Point('o')
p1_pt = Point('p1_pt')
p2_pt = Point('p2_pt')
p1_pt.set_pos(o, 1 * N.x)
p2_pt.set_pos(o, 2 * N.x)
m = symbols('m')
p1 = Particle('p1', p1_pt, m)
p2 = Particle('p2', p2_pt, -m)
print(center_of_mass(o, p1, p2))
```

Output:--
```
zoo*m*N.x + nan*N.y + nan*N.z
```

Expected:--
```
ValueError: Total mass of the system is zero, center of mass is undefined.
```

Tested on:- sympy==1.14.0

issues.title: [FEATURE]: AI-powered "Chat with your Quiz" — ask follow-up questions on quiz content
issues.body:
### Feature and its Use Cases

## Problem
Currently, EduAid generates quizzes from content but provides no way 
for users to interact with that content further. When a user gets a 
question wrong or doesn't understand an answer, there is no way to 
ask "why is this the correct answer?" or "explain this concept in 
simpler terms". Users have to go back to the original source manually.

## Proposed Solution
Add a conversational AI layer on top of the generated quiz that allows 
users to:

- Ask follow-up questions like "explain why option B is correct"
- Ask "give me an example of this concept"
- Ask "summarize this topic in simple terms"
- Chat directly with an uploaded PDF — ask questions about its content 
  beyond just the generated quiz

## How It Works

### Chat with Quiz
1. When a quiz is generated, the source content is stored as context
2. User can open a chat panel alongside the quiz
3. User asks a question — it gets matched against the source content 
   using semantic search (RAG)
4. A language model generates a grounded answer from the source material

### Chat with PDF
1. User uploads a PDF
2. PDF text is extracted and chunked
3. Chunks are embedded and stored in a FAISS vector index
4. User can ask any question about the PDF content
5. Relevant chunks are retrieved and passed to the LLM for a grounded answer

## Tech Stack Suggestion
- Python, FAISS, Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
- LLM: Google Gemini API or local open-source model
- Frontend: existing React/JS chat UI pattern

## Why This Fits EduAid
EduAid's goal is to help students learn and retain information. 
A chat interface transforms EduAid from a passive quiz tool into 
an active learning companion — students don't just get tested, 
they get to understand. This is a natural and powerful extension 
of the existing quiz generation feature.

## Additional Notes
I have prior experience building a RAG pipeline from scratch 
(DocuMind — PDF Q&A using FAISS + Gemini) and would love to 
implement this feature. Happy to discuss the approach before 
starting.

### Additional Context

_No response_

- [x] I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates

issues.title: Feat: AI-powered natural language search for photos
issues.body: ### Describe the feature ## Problem Currently, PictoPy doesn't provide any special search feature. There is no way to search using natural, conversational queries like "show me photos from the beach" or "find pictures with my friends from last year". ## Proposed Solution Implement a natural language search feature powered by AI that allows users to query their photo library conversationally. This would involve: - Generating text embeddings for each photo's metadata (tags, detected objects, faces, dates, locations) - Storing embeddings in a vector index (e.g. FAISS) - Accepting natural language queries from the user - Converting queries to embeddings and retrieving the most semantically relevant photos ## Additional AI Features (can be separate issues) - **Duplicate photo detection** — identify near-identical images using embedding similarity and allow bulk removal - **Auto-generated albums** — automatically group photos by event, location, or date using clustering ## Why This Fits PictoPy PictoPy already uses YOLO and FaceNet to extract rich metadata from photos. This feature would make that metadata actually queryable in a natural way, significantly improving the user experience without compromising the privacy-first approach since all processing stays local. ## Tech Stack Suggestion - Python, FAISS, Sentence Transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 for lightweight local embeddings) - No external API calls — fully offline and privacy-preserving ## Additional Notes I'm interested in working on this feature and have prior experience building RAG pipelines (FAISS + embeddings). Happy to discuss the approach before starting implementation. ### Add ScreenShots ### Record - [x] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct - [x] I want to work on this issue

issues.title: [FEATURE]: Footer lacks information about AOSSIE and useful project links
issues.body:
### Feature and its Use Cases

The current footer only shows a Discord icon and the text "Made with love by AOSSIE team". Visitors who are unfamiliar with AOSSIE have no context about the organisation behind PictoPy, and there are no links to key resources like documentation, the GitHub repo, release downloads, or community channels.

Expected behaviour

The footer should:

Include a brief description of AOSSIE and a link to aossie.org
Provide quick links to Docs, Releases, Issue Tracker, and Contributing Guide
Surface community links (Discord, GitHub, Twitter)
Show a proper copyright notice with the current year
Current behaviour

Footer is a single line: Discord icon + "Made with love by AOSSIE team"

Suggested solution
Replace with a responsive multi-column layout that covers the brand, organisation background, quick links, and community — consistent with the existing yellow→green design system and dark mode support.

issues.title: Add testing framework for meshery.io
issues.body:
### Current Behavior
The meshery.io website currently has no testing infrastructure. The [package.json](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) test script outputs "Error: no test specified" and there are no unit tests, HTML validation, or CI workflows for testing.

### Desired Behavior
Add a testing framework to improve code quality and catch issues before deployment:

JavaScript Unit Testing: Add Jest to test utility functions in [blog-search.js](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) (slugify, escapeRegex, highlightText, debounce, search functions)
HTML/Link Validation: Add html-proofer gem to validate internal links and HTML structure
CI Integration: Add GitHub Actions workflow to run tests automatically on PRs
Makefile Targets: Add convenient commands like make test, make test-js, make test-html, make setup

This would help catch bugs in search functionality and broken links before deployment, and improve the contributor experience.

Screenshots / Mockups
N/A - This is a developer tooling enhancement.

I'd be happy to work on this if a maintainer approves. Please assign this to me if it looks good!

issues.title: Add optional REST API and Web UI for ScrapPY
issues.body:
**Description**

This PR introduces an optional, decoupled REST API and web-based interface for ScrapPY, designed with strict adherence to OWASP security principles.

**Motivation**

Enable controlled, auditable access to ScrapPY functionality
Provide explicit consent requirements before any scan
Add authentication, authorization, and rate limiting by default
Support deployment in enterprise/team environments


**Security Features**

✅ No unsafe defaults - Secret key required via environment variable
✅ Authentication required - JWT tokens for all operations
✅ Rate limiting - Configurable limits on login and job submission
✅ Explicit consent - Checkbox/flag required before every scan
✅ Input validation - File size limits, PDF magic byte verification
✅ Isolation - ScrapPY runs as subprocess, not imported

**Non-Goals (Explicitly Excluded)**

No modification to ScrapPY core logic
No auto-execution without user consent
No GUI embedded into ScrapPY core
